outcome_text: Feedback processes for end users and impacted communities to report problems and appeal system outcomes are established and integrated into AI system evaluation metrics.
scoring_text: Skill in designing end-user and community feedback mechanisms for AI systems, including establishing problem reporting channels, creating outcome appeal processes, and integrating feedback data into AI system evaluation metrics and risk tracking.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing end-user and community feedback mechanisms for AI systems, including establishing problem reporting channels, creating outcome appeal processes, and integrating feedback data into AI system evaluation metrics and risk tracking.
reference_document_element_description: Execute bias audits on AI systems in development and production, documenting methodology, findings, and recommended interventions.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-3.3 (MEASURE) intersects with WIDAI AG domain. AG-T-009 is the strongest match (STS: 0.407, cosine: 0.653). Secondary: AG-S-006 (AG, cosine: 0.626), RM-T-004 (RM, cosine: 0.624).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.1/10 (raw 0.407). Shared vocabulary: [ai, systems]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [appeal, channels, community, creating, data, designing] vs WIDAI terms [audits, bias, development, documenting, execute, findings] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.872, R=0.888). Balanced P/R — the 32-word competency implication and 16-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (94.1%). Semantic tension between "Skill in designing end-user and community feedback mechanisms for AI systems, in" and "Execute bias audits on AI systems in development and production, documenting met". Despite STS-based 'Intersects with' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.653 > STS 0.407 (gap +0.246). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, systems] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, systems]. NIST AI RMF-only: [appeal, channels, community, creating, data, designing]. WIDAI-only: [audits, bias, development, documenting, execute, findings].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 94.1% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.246 with low STS 0.407. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
